corrections.text: The article puts inside quote marks the phrase 'on a new alien world where players are stranded after their colony ship experiences a malfunction during transit' attributed to Engadget. That exact wording does not appear in the Engadget piece. Engadget says 'Subnautica 2 takes place on a new world' and then quotes the game's official in-universe description: 'Driven from your home by ongoing conflict, Alterra offers you the chance at a new life. But as the colony ship CICADA shepherds you and your fellow Pioneers to your new home, something goes awry. The ship's AI insists that your mission should continue. Stranded and faced with near-insurmountable odds, you must do everything in your power to survive.' The substance — new world, colony-ship plot setup, players are stranded — is accurate, but the quote-marked phrase is a paraphrase, not verbatim Engadget text. Either the quote marks should be removed or the wording should match the Engadget passage (or the game's official description) verbatim.
